perturbation.original_text: The amount is payable 180 days from the date of the Event.
perturbation.changed_text: The amount is payable within 180 days from the date of the Event.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies the payment date exactly, whereas the modified version introduces a range. This is contradictory to section 9 stating this agreement is the entire agreement and supersedes all prior agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement of the parties hereto with regard to the subject matter hereof and supersedes and replaces all prior agreements, understandings and representations, oral or written, with regard to such matter.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a precise payment date (180 days from the event), ensuring uniformity in agreement terms. The change introduces a range ('within 180 days'), conflicting with the 'Contradicted Text,' which mandates that the current Agreement supersedes all prior agreements and represents the entire understanding, including definite obligations. The modified text creates ambiguity, directly undermining the purpose of the 'entire agreement' clause, therefore it introduces ambiguities, and is an In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Display materials from the Sponsor must be delivered to the Tian He Stadium at least two days prior to the event.
perturbation.changed_text: Display materials from the Sponsor should be delivered to the Tian He Stadium approximately two days prior to the event.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'must' to 'should' weakens the obligation. This is contradictory to section 7 stating that all agreements are binding upon the successors and assigns.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All covenants, promises and agreements by or on behalf of the parties contained in this Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of the successors and assigns of the parties; but nothing in this Agreement, expressed or implied is intended to confer on any party the right to assign its rights or obligations hereunder. Nothing in this Agreement, whether expressed or implied, is intended to confer any rights or remedies under or by reason of this Agreement on any persons other than the parties to it and their respective successors and assigns, nor is anything in this Agreement intended to relieve or discharge the obligation or liability of any third persons to any party to this Agreement, nor shall any provision give any third person any right of subrogation or action over or against any party to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'must', creating a binding obligation for the Sponsor to deliver materials two days prior to the event. The changed text uses 'should', which weakens this obligation and introduces ambiguity ('approximately'). This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that 'all covenants, promises and agreements... shall be binding', thus, introducing a strong in-text contradiction as there is no binding obligation per the change in the altered text. The change now makes the 'agreement' that display materials should be delivered unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a dispute, the parties shall seek mediation at a third country mutually agreed upon.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a dispute, the parties may seek mediation at a location mutually agreed upon.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall seek mediation at a third country' to 'may seek mediation at a location' introduces ambiguity. This is contradictory to section 9 stating this agreement is the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement of the parties hereto with regard to the subject matter hereof and supersedes and replaces all prior agreements, understandings and representations, oral or written, with regard to such matter.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated mediation at a specific third-country location, implying a defined dispute resolution process and certainty. The changed text makes mediation optional ('may seek') and removes the specific location requirement, introducing ambiguity. This directly conflicts with the 'entire agreement' clause, which establishes that the contract represents the complete understanding and supersedes prior agreements. The change undermines the definiteness of the dispute resolution process, which the 'entire agreement' clause intended to solidify, therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
